Sense.Definition: action happening in the present, en route to
proclaim upon something or someone; pray over something or someone
a word used before or after the number of times something occurred; frequency
sit with legs folded in(humans); curl body together in a rest position(reptiles)
To curl body in rest position
(a heavy or bulky object) be kept or placed somewhere; a body of water collected somewhere
used to identify a person or thing that is close at hand, indicated, or experienced; this
a word that is used to join two nouns in a phrase or sentence; coordinating conjunction
the singular, subject, third-person pronoun of the Igbo language (heavy vowel harmonizer); he; she; it
denotes past with action verbs and present with non-alternating stative verbs
be empty-handed; be left with nothing
(especially animal or human) catch; grip; hold in such a way as to not give room for freedom (likely humiliating, especially for humans)
first person plural pronoun equivalent of first person singular object pronoun which can be used as a subject
how often; how many times; the number of times
used to seek forgiveness or permission; please (beg);
an expression of negative outcome, disagreement or refusal; no
to be withered; to be shrivelled
we; us; our; first-person Igbo language plural pronoun
an expression of agreement to comply with a command or request; I or we will
indicates continuing action in the present
be equal to, on an equality; be as much
have nothing in one's hand, be empty-handed
an expression positive, agreement to, or acceptance of what was said; yes
in the past, formerly, not now
it is; he is; she is
carry or bear something big or heavy, especially as a load
up to a point in time; until; till
let; so that; that; like; as, while; do, is(after a question tag before a subject)
indicate continuing action in the present-
in, on, at; to(optional); is(before a continuous verb)
have a wear on; wear; put on (a wear)
for the sake; hold as a reason
completive or open vowel suffix, used with most verbs in the imperative, the subjunctive, and the sequential forms
stay or sit u pon; stay put somewhere or on something
be at or in a place(heavy or bulky object) ; stay (heavy or bulky object)
first of all; beforehand; a sec

Synset.Definition: action happening in the present, en route to
proclaim upon something or someone; pray over something or someone
a word used before or after the number of times something occurred; frequency
sit with legs folded in(humans); curl body together in a rest position(reptiles)

used to identify a person or thing that is close at hand, indicated, or experienced; this

have a wear on; wear; put on (a wear)
for the sake; hold as a reason
completive or open vowel suffix, used with most verbs in the imperative, the subjunctive, and the sequential forms
stay or sit u pon; stay put somewhere or on something
